plan: no action: unix_time_ms() body is split into cfg(not(madsim)) (real wall clock) and cfg(madsim) (panic with explanation); SystemWallClock under cfg(madsim) panics on use, forcing every HttpState caller to inject WallClock via with_wall_clock(_handle)

no action: cold_object_unix_nanos() is split — cfg(not(madsim)) calls SystemTime::now(); cfg(madsim) returns 0. Cold object uniqueness under cfg(madsim) is provided by COLD_CHUNK_SEQUENCE, and reset_cold_chunk_sequence_for_sim() lets the simulator clear the counter between scenarios for Runtime::check_determinism replay.

no action: unix_time_ms() is split — cfg(not(madsim)) reads the real wall clock for cold-compaction GC grace, while cfg(madsim) returns 0 so simulation scheduling and replicated commands remain deterministic.

no action: the call is inside #[cfg(not(madsim))] mod s3; under cfg(madsim), snapshot_store_from_env rejects the s3 backend and only inline snapshots are available, so the wall-clock nonce is not sim-reachable.
no action: gc_wall_clock_now() uses the real wall clock only under cfg(not(madsim)); cfg(madsim) returns UNIX_EPOCH, and garbage_collect accepts an injected SystemTime for deterministic tests.

no action: paired with the tokio_runtime_builder entry above; the surrounding match arm and the enum variant are both cfg(not(madsim))-gated

no action: the call sits inside #[cfg(not(madsim))] async fn sleep_in_process_raft_network and cannot reach the simulator; whitelist entry exists so the audit script doesn't false-positive on it

no action: ursula-ctl is a host-side CLI; the DST harness only consumes ursula_ctl::plan + ursula_ctl::metrics (see crates/ursula-sim/src/madsim_harness/rolling_restart.rs). The Instant-based wait loops in observe.rs (collect_status timeout) are exercised exclusively by the ursulactl binary, never by madsim scenarios.

DST reaches ursula-ctl through ursula_ctl::plan helpers only, which take pre-built ClusterSnapshot/NodeMetricsView arguments and don't touch wall-clock time.
